layman_headline: Extreme fear grips crypto markets — patience beats impulse right now.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in 'Extreme Fear' mode with a score of just 22/100, meaning most people are scared and selling. Bitcoin just lost $424 million from ETFs and geopolitical tensions between the U.S. and Iran are adding pressure. While fear can create buying opportunities, none of the coins on our watchlist present a clean, high-conviction setup with good risk/reward right now. The best candidates are either already overbought (LIT at RSI 70), have weak chart structures (GRASS, MON below all moving averages), or lack sufficient data (XPL).
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. The market is fearful but hasn't shown a clear reversal signal yet. Watch for ETH or a quality alt to form a proper bottom with volume confirmation before entering.
expert_reasoning: I evaluated all 7 swing candidates and found no high-confidence setup. MON (RSI 36.8) is the most oversold but has a neutral chart bias, is below all MAs, and the risk/reward to nearest support/resistance is only 0.79x — below our 2x threshold. GRASS dropped 27% in 7 days which is a mean-reversion signal, but its chart bias is bearish (-0.45) and it's below all MAs with poor R/R of 0.69x. LIT has strong 5-day momentum (+53%) but RSI at 70 is overbought — chasing here violates our discipline. JTO's RSI at 59 contradicts its -17% weekly drop, suggesting the bounce may already be priced in. BTC dominance at 56% continues to crush alts. With ETF outflows of $424M and U.S.-Iran escalation creating macro headwinds, the prudent move is to wait for a cleaner setup.
risk_factors: U.S.-Iran geopolitical escalation could trigger a broader risk-off sell-off across crypto
Bitcoin ETF outflows of $424M signal institutional distribution that may continue
BTC dominance at 56% continues to squeeze altcoin valuations — entering alts prematurely is a capital trap
watch_list: ETH price action — if it holds above its 20-day SMA after today's 5.4% surge, it could signal a legitimate reversal worth entering
Fear & Greed Index trend — currently improving from 28 to 22 (wait, actually declining). Watch for a bottoming pattern below 20 or a sharp reversal above 30
BTC reaction to $65,000 resistance — a breakout could lift the entire market and create clean alt setups
market_context: Crypto markets are gripped by extreme fear (22/100) amid U.S.-Iran tensions and $424M in Bitcoin ETF outflows. BTC holds $63.6K but altcoins are bleeding with high BTC dominance at 56%.
context_banner: Extreme fear without a clear technical bottom — capital preservation is the highest-edge play right now.

key_signals.description: Extreme Fear at 22/100 suggests potential contrarian opportunity, but the trend is still deteriorating from last week's 23.9 average — not yet bottomed.
Bitcoin ETFs lost $424M in outflows, wiping out last week's gains — institutional money is exiting, removing a key bid from the market.
U.S.-Iran escalation is weighing on bitcoin and stocks as oil climbs, adding macro headwinds to an already fragile market.
Best candidate MON has RSI 36.8 (approaching oversold) but chart structure is bearish with price below all MAs and poor 0.79x risk/reward ratio.
U.S. June CPI fell 0.4%, which is moderately positive for risk assets but not enough to offset geopolitical headwinds in the near term.
